stories.headline: Trump Sees Iran Deal as Imminent, Nuclear Issue Remains in Limbo
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said a deal with Iran to end the seven-week war may be imminent though disagreements remain, with Tehran pushing back against US claims that it had agreed to give up its enriched uranium.

stories.headline: Iran war live: Tehran says US blockade of ports must end as strait opens
stories.summary: Tens of thousands of people forcibly displaced by Israeli attacks on Lebanon use first day of ceasefire to return home.

stories.headline: Oil prices plunge as Iran says Strait 'open'
stories.summary: Brent crude sinks by a tenth after Iran says the key waterway is open for commercial ships for the rest of the ceasefire.

stories.headline: Iran says Strait of Hormuz will 'not remain open' if US blockade continues
stories.summary: A fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran showed signs of strain Saturday as Tehran threatened to re-close the Strait of Hormuz over an ongoing American naval blockade.

They were all waiting to see Dr Mirela Csabai, one of just seven general practitioners serving a population of more than 30,000.Most of the cases that morning were routine: colds, checkups, chronic conditions. stories.summary: Rise in domestic tourism could provide relief for rural hospitality businesses struggling with higher costs

stories.summary: Company expands accommodation options alongside home rentals but analysts warn of stiff competition

stories.headline: UK Bears Scars to Economy, Trump Ties Even as War Risks Subside

stories.summary: Officials plan to reopen frontier with Armenia to unlock a Trump-backed trade route between Europe and Asia

stories.headline: Odd Lots: Why Economists Might Be Getting AI Wrong (Podcast)
stories.summary: Everyone knows that new technologies can be really disruptive to the labor market, but eventually new jobs emerge and things come back into balance. And there is a sense in which many view AI with the same lens. Yes, there will be pain in some sectors, but then there will be productivity gains and new sources of demand and new opportunities for labor that we can’t conceive of yet. But could it be different this time? Could AI be disruptive in a m

stories.headline: Bangladesh Continues Talks with IMF on Key Reforms Tied to Loan
stories.summary: Bangladesh said it will continue negotiating with the International Monetary Fund on key reforms the IMF has set as conditions for the balance of a $5.5 billion loan, with Finance Minister Amir Khosru Mahmud Chowdhury holding meetings in Washington with the global lender’s officials.

stories.headline: Greek, Indian Tankers U-Turn Before Hormuz Amid Reopening Doubt
stories.summary: Several oil tankers have u-turned in the Persian Gulf after appearing to try to transit the Strait of Hormuz, as shipowners and oil traders remain in a state of disarray as they try to figure out whether Iran would stick to its promise to keep the chokepoint open for all.

stories.headline: Australian Minister Says Fuel Reserves Up, More Shipments on Way
stories.summary: Australia’s fuel reserves rose in the past week and more is on the way, Energy Minister Chris Bowen said, easing concern of shortages in the import-reliant country.

stories.headline: Fermi Shares Plunge After Announcing Departure of CEO
stories.summary: Fermi shares fell as much as 31% in post-market trading after the developer behind a massive planned AI campus in Texas announced the immediate departure of co-founder and Chief Executive Officer Toby Neugebauer.

stories.headline: Trump’s Dispute With Pope Leo Deepens Divisions on the Right
stories.summary: Sean Hannity criticized the pope. Tucker Carlson attacked Mr. Hannity. And President Trump suggested ranking MAGA figures: “good, bad, and somewhere in the middle.”

stories.headline: With Vaccines Widely Popular, Kennedy Changes Tone, but Maybe Not His Plans
stories.summary: Several moves suggest Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. could revive his campaign to question the safety and effectiveness of the shots after the midterm elections.

stories.headline: LNG Ships Move Toward Hormuz After Iran Says Strait Is Open
stories.summary: Several liquefied natural gas tankers appear to be nearing the Strait of Hormuz, boosting optimism that flows of the super-chilled fuel through the waterway may resume after Iran said it was open.

stories.headline: Oil Tankers Make Dash Toward Hormuz as Iran Says Strait Is Open
stories.summary: Iran says the Strait of Hormuz is open — and oil tankers are racing through it. Norah Mulinda explains how markets reacted to the news and asks: Can this lead to a lasting peace agreement? (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Ceasefire with Israel brings respite to Lebanon, but obstacles to peace remain
stories.summary: The halt to weeks of fighting is being celebrated in Lebanon, but it will be difficult to ensure it lasts.

stories.headline: Iran rejects Trump claim on deal to surrender nuclear material stockpiles

stories.headline: Lebanon president says country is no longer a pawn amid Israel ceasefire
stories.summary: President Joseph Aoun promised to work to preserve Lebanon's sovereignty and freedom.

stories.headline: Oil prices drop over 10% after Iran declares the Strait of Hormuz 'completely open'
stories.summary: Oil prices have dropped over 10% in less than two hours after the Minister for Foreign Affairs of Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz "completely open" for the remainder of the current ceasefire with the US.

stories.headline: Oil flows from Russia via Druzhba pipeline to Hungary could resume next week, Magyar says
stories.summary: The pipeline, which delivers Russian oil to Hungary via Ukraine, has been a source of contention between Budapest and Kyiv after flows were stopped when it sustained damage in a drone strike.

stories.headline: Suspected crime boss Daniel Kinahan arrested in Dubai
stories.summary: Kinahan, in his 40s, was arrested in Dubai on foot of an arrest warrant issued by the Irish courts.

stories.summary: EQT AB, Europe’s biggest private equity firm, says the path to exiting investments in clean-energy developers and operators faces a growing number of hurdles.

stories.headline: Oil Tankers Hauling US Crude Via Panama Approach 4-Year High
stories.summary: US oil cargoes transiting the Panama Canal are close to a four-year high as Asian refiners rush to import American crude in lieu of Mideast supplies strangled by the weeks-long disruption to Strait of Hormuz shipping.

stories.headline: Supreme Court Sides With Oil Companies in Louisiana Coastal Lawsuits
stories.summary: The companies had asked the justices to clear the way to move environmental lawsuits out of state courts, to friendlier federal venues.

stories.summary: Experts say Mythos potentially has an unprecedented ability to identify and exploit cyber-security weaknesses.

stories.summary: A 13-hour blaze at one of Australia's two oil refineries could impact petrol prices and supply.

stories.headline: The Starmer-shaped hole where a prime minister should be

stories.headline: Blue Owl Co-CEOs’ Personal Loans No Longer Backed by Firm Shares
stories.summary: Blue Owl Capital Inc. Co-Chief Executive Officers Doug Ostrover and Marc Lipschultz have revised the terms of personal loans to remove the company’s shares as collateral, after turmoil in the private credit market hammered the stock’s value in recent months.

stories.summary: Lauren Berger and Marla Felton, REAL Cookies Co-Founders joined Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss their merger with Creations Foods and the outlook for healthy snacking. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Pressure mounts on Peru’s election authorities amid presidential race delay
stories.summary: The vote count continues to determine who will join conservative Keiko Fujimori in Peru's presidential run-off in June.

stories.headline: Venezuela and EU set out roadmap for renewed ties amid political thaw
stories.summary: Following talks in the Venezuelan parliament, Caracas and the EU have agreed on a new cooperation agenda. The shift aligns with a broader economic pivot, as the nation resumes ties with the IMF and the World Bank.

stories.headline: Japan pledges $10bn to help Asian countries deal with oil crisis
stories.summary: The aid is roughly equivalent to a year's worth of crude oil imports by Asean countries.

stories.headline: Naples bank robbers hold 25 people hostage then vanish through tunnel
stories.summary: The armed men reportedly evaded capture by escaping through the city's sewer system.

stories.headline: UK seeks closer EU ties in volatile times - but at what cost?
stories.summary: The UK is adopting a "ruthlessly pragmatic" approach to becoming closer to its European neighbours, the UK's EU minister tells the BBC.

stories.headline: Spain approves plan to give around 500,000 undocumented migrants legal status
